title: विट्ठल स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विट्ठल स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
कटिकरं विट्ठलं भजे पण्ढरीनाथम्।
ईट्यां स्थितं चन्द्रभागातीरे रुक्मिणीनाथम्॥

॥ स्तोत्रम् ॥
नमो विट्ठल देवाय पण्ढरीपुरवासिने।
भक्तवत्सल कृष्णाय कटिहस्ताय ते नमः॥ १॥

चन्द्रभागातटे रम्ये ईट्यां तिष्ठसि सादरम्।
पुण्डलीकवरप्रद देव विट्ठल नमोऽस्तु ते॥ २॥

तुकाराम नमदेव चोखामेळा सखा प्रभो।
सन्तसंगति दातार विट्ठल नमोऽस्तु ते॥ ३॥

ज्ञानदेव विशेषेण भक्तिमार्गप्रकाशक।
वारकरी जनोद्धार विट्ठल नमोऽस्तु ते॥ ४॥

विठू माउली तू माझा सर्वस्व तू विठोबा।
मायबाप तू एकची नमो पण्ढरीबा॥ ५॥

आषाढी कार्तिकी वारी पण्ढरपुरा जाय।
दिण्डी पालखी घेऊनी विट्ठल नाम गाय॥ ६॥

अभंग कीर्तन भजन भक्तिरस सागरम्।
गोपालकृष्णस्वरूपं विट्ठलं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ७॥

रुक्मिणी सहित विट्ठल दोन्ही कटेवरी कर।
भीमा तीरी उभा रहे भक्ता चा कैवार॥ ८॥

पण्ढरीचे वाळूवंट तीर्थक्षेत्र सुशोभितम्।
महाद्वारी गरुडस्तम्भ विट्ठलं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ९॥

विठ्ठल विठ्ठल जय हरि विठ्ठल।
पण्ढरीनाथ विठ्ठल माउली विठ्ठल।
रुक्मिणीवल्लभ विठ्ठल जयदेव विठ्ठल।
भक्तवत्सल विठ्ठल नमो नमो विठ्ठल॥ १०॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
विट्ठलस्तोत्रमेतत्तु यः पठेत् भक्तिसंयुतः।
पण्ढरीक्षेत्रवासस्य फलमाप्नोति मानवः॥

भक्तिमार्गं लभेत् सद्यः कृष्णप्रेम सुदुर्लभम्।
विट्ठलस्य प्रसादेन सर्वकामार्थसिद्धये॥

॥ इति श्री विट्ठल स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vitthal Stotram ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Kati-karam Vitthalam Bhaje Pandhari-natham.
Itya Sthitam Chandra-bhaga-tire Rukmini-natham.

|| Stotram ||
Namo Vitthal Devaya Pandhari-pura-vasine.
Bhakta-vatsala Krishnaya Kati-hastaya Te Namah. || 1 ||

Chandra-bhaga-tate Ramye Ityam Tishthasi Sadaram.
Pundalika-vara-prada Deva Vitthal Namo'stu Te. || 2 ||

Tukaram Namdev Chokhamela Sakha Prabho.
Sant-sangati Datar Vitthal Namo'stu Te. || 3 ||

Jnanadev Visheshena Bhakti-marga-prakashaka.
Varkari Jana-uddhara Vitthal Namo'stu Te. || 4 ||

Vithu Mauli Tu Majha Sarvasva Tu Vithoba.
Maybap Tu Ekchi Namo Pandhariba. || 5 ||

Ashadhi Kartiki Vari Pandharpur Jaay.
Dindi Palakhi Gheuni Vitthal Nam Gaay. || 6 ||

Abhang Kirtan Bhajan Bhakti-rasa Sagaram.
Gopala-Krishna-svarupam Vitthalam Pranamamyaham. || 7 ||

Rukmini Sahit Vitthal Donhi Katevari Kar.
Bhima Tiri Ubha Rahe Bhakta Cha Kaivar. || 8 ||

Pandharice Valuvant Tirtha-kshetra Sushobhitam.
Mahadwari Garuda-stambha Vitthalam Pranamamyaham. || 9 ||

Vitthal Vitthal Jai Hari Vitthal.
Pandhari-nath Vitthal Mauli Vitthal.
Rukmini-vallabh Vitthal Jaidev Vitthal.
Bhakta-vatsala Vitthal Namo Namo Vitthal. || 10 ||

|| Phalashruti ||
Vitthal-stotram-etat-tu Yah Pathet Bhakti-samyutah.
Pandhari-kshetra-vasasya Phalam-aapnoti Manavah.

Bhakti-margam Labhet Sadyah Krishna-prema Su-durlabham.
Vitthalasya Prasadena Sarva-kama-artha-siddhaye.

|| Iti Shri Vitthal Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
कटि पर हाथ रखे पण्ढरीनाथ विट्ठल का भजन करता हूँ, जो ईंट पर खड़े हैं, चन्द्रभागा (भीमा) नदी तट पर, रुक्मिणी के नाथ हैं।

पण्ढरीपुरवासी, भक्तवत्सल, कृष्ण स्वरूप, कटि पर हाथ रखे विट्ठल देव को नमन। चन्द्रभागा के रम्य तट पर ईंट पर खड़े, पुण्डलीक को वर देने वाले!

तुकाराम, नामदेव, चोखामेळा के सखा! ज्ञानदेव विशेष रूप से भक्तिमार्ग के प्रकाशक! वारकरी जनों का उद्धार करने वाले! विठू माउली (माता), सर्वस्व, माय-बाप!

आषाढी और कार्तिकी एकादशी पर वारी (यात्रा) पण्ढरपुर जाती है, दिण्डी-पालखी लेकर विट्ठल नाम गाते हैं। अभंग, कीर्तन, भजन का भक्तिरस सागर! गोपालकृष्ण स्वरूप विट्ठल को प्रणाम।

जो भक्तिपूर्वक इस स्तोत्र का पाठ करता है, उसे पण्ढरी क्षेत्र वास का फल मिलता है, भक्तिमार्ग और दुर्लभ कृष्णप्रेम प्राप्त होता है।
benefits: Invokes the merit of pandharpur pilgrimage
Invokes the blessing of krishna-prema and deep devotion (bhakti)
Connects with the Varkari devotional tradition
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of wishes through vitthal's grace
Especially powerful on Ashadhi and Kartiki Ekadashi
Purifies the heart through the bhakti rasa of abhang tradition
description: Vitthal Stotram (विट्ठल स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Lord Vitthal. Rooted in Varkari Sant Paramapara, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Devotees recite this prayer to purify the heart through the bhakti rasa of abhang tradition and grant the merit of Pandharpur pilgrimage. It is also said to connect with the Varkari devotional tradition. This prayer is traditionally recited on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Vaikuntha Ekadashi, Diwali, and Govardhan Puja to invoke divine grace. Comprising 10 verses, it can be completed in approximately 10 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Vitthal Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Vitthal and where is the chief Vitthal temple located?
seoFaqs.answer: Lord Vitthal (also called Vithoba, Pandurang, Panduranga, Vithu Mauli) is a deeply beloved form of Lord Krishna-Vishnu worshipped chiefly in Maharashtra and northern Karnataka, with his principal temple at Pandharpur on the banks of the Bhima river (locally called Chandrabhaga because it bends in the shape of a half-moon). The murti depicts a youthful, dark-complexioned form standing on a brick (vit) with both hands resting on his hips (kati-hasta) — a posture said to commemorate the moment when he came to bless his devotee Pundalika who was busy serving his elderly parents. He is the focal deity of the Varkari Sampradaya, the great Marathi bhakti tradition. (Sources: Pandharpur temple tradition; Skanda Purana Pandurangamahatmya.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the story of Pundalik and the brick on which Vitthal stands?
seoFaqs.answer: The most beloved Pandharpur legend tells how Lord Vishnu came in the form of Vitthal to bless his devotee Pundalik, a young man who was so absorbed in serving his elderly parents that when the Lord arrived at his door, he simply tossed a brick (vit) outside and asked the Lord to wait on it until his service to his parents was complete. The Lord, charmed by this perfect filial devotion (matru-pitru-bhakti), stood patiently on the brick with his hands on his hips — and remained standing there ever since for all his devotees. The very name Vitthal is etymologised as Vit-thala — one who stands on a brick — and the iconic kati-hasta pose memorialises this divine patience. (Source: Pandurangamahatmya; Varkari Sampradaya tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Varkari Sampradaya and how is the Vitthal Stotram connected to it?
seoFaqs.answer: The Varkari Sampradaya is the great bhakti movement of Maharashtra centred on devotion to Vitthal of Pandharpur, founded and shaped by the legendary sant-poets Sant Jnanadev (13th century, author of the Jnaneshwari), Sant Namdev, Sant Eknath, Sant Tukaram, Sant Chokhamela and Sant Janabai — collectively called the Varkari santas. Twice a year, on Ashadhi Ekadashi (June-July) and Kartiki Ekadashi (October-November), millions of devotees walk hundreds of kilometres in the Pandharpur Vari pilgrimage, carrying palkhis with the padukas of the saints, singing abhangas, dindi-style. The Vitthal Stotram explicitly invokes Tukaram, Namdev, Chokhamela and Jnanadev by name and salutes the entire Varkari path of bhakti. (Source: Varkari Sampradaya history; Pandharpur Vari tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Vitthal Stotram traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vitthal Stotram is most powerful on Ashadhi Ekadashi (Devshayani Ekadashi, the principal Pandharpur Vari) and Kartiki Ekadashi (Prabodhini/Devuthani Ekadashi, the second great Vari), on every Ekadashi (sacred to Vishnu), every Thursday (Guruvar — Vishnu's day) and Wednesday (Budhvar — sometimes associated with Krishna). It is especially recited on the seven days leading up to and during the Vari pilgrimage, on Vaikuntha Ekadashi, on Krishna Janmashtami, during Diwali, on Govardhan Puja, and as nityapaath by Varkari devotees. Pilgrims chant it walking in dindi processions to Pandharpur with cymbals and mridanga. (Sources: Varkari Vari paddhati; Drikpanchang Ekadashi calendar.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Vitthal Stotram bestow according to its phalashruti?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the Vitthal Stotram declares that the devotee who recites this stotra with bhakti attains: (1) Pandhari-kshetra-vasasya phalam — the merit equivalent to actually living and dying at the holy Pandharpur kshetra (one of the great moksha-puris), (2) bhakti-margam labhet sadyah — immediate establishment on the path of bhakti, (3) Krishna-prema su-durlabham — the rare and supreme gift of pure love for Krishna, and (4) sarva-kama-artha-siddhaye — fulfilment of all righteous desires through Vitthal's grace. Vitthal is considered the supreme grantor of bhakti-rasa, and his Stotram is especially efficacious for those who cannot physically undertake the Vari pilgrimage. (Source: Vitthal Stotram phalashruti.)

seoFaqs.question: Why does Vitthal stand with both hands on his hips, and what does the iconography mean?
seoFaqs.answer: The kati-hasta posture (both hands resting on the hips) is the most iconic feature of the Vitthal murti at Pandharpur and is rich in theological meaning. Tradition gives several layered explanations: (1) it commemorates the patience with which Vitthal waited on Pundalik's brick, (2) it is said to indicate that the sea of samsara reaches only up to the hips of those who take refuge in him — that is, his devotees can cross it easily, (3) the relaxed, casual posture signifies that he is wholly accessible (sulabha) to the simplest devotee, with no formal mudra, no weapons displayed, just pure standing presence, and (4) it is read as Vitthal saying — I am simply here, come to me. (Sources: Pandurangamahatmya; Sant Tukaram abhangas; Varkari iconographic tradition.)
